name: RRB Tree (Relaxed Radix Balanced)

x.invariant: a wide (radix 32) balanced tree of array chunks; "relaxed" nodes carry a size table so concat/split need not perfectly fill nodes.
x.memoryOverhead: Leaf chunks of ~32 elements plus internal index nodes; structural sharing across versions.
x.locality: Good within a 32-element leaf chunk; tree walk between chunks.
x.mutationModel: Persistent immutable vector; supports fast functional concat/slice that a plain persistent vector cannot.
x.concurrency: Immutable snapshots share freely across threads; transients accelerate batch building.

def: A persistent indexed sequence (immutable vector) that adds efficient concatenation and slicing on top of the radix-balanced trie used for functional vectors.
problem: An immutable list with near-O(1) random access AND fast concat/split — the two are hard to get together.
mechanism: Stores elements in radix-32 leaf chunks under a balanced tree; "relaxed" nodes keep a size table so concatenated subtrees need not be perfectly full, making concat/slice logarithmic.
limits: Larger constants than a flat dynamic array
failures: Naive rebalancing can degrade to deep trees
whenNot: Mutable single-owner sequence (use a dynamic array)
myths: "Functional vectors can’t concat fast" — RRB makes it O(log n)
